name: ElmChat

unions.name: CustomRenderers
unions.comment:
Used to customize rendering.

Set the `customRenderers` field of your settings record to one of these, if you want to override the default rendering, or render your own CustomLineSpec.

The `overrider` can provide an alternate to the default renderers for `StringLineSpec`, `UserLineSpec`, and `TimeLineSpec`.

The `renderer` will be called on `CustomLineSpec`s.
cases: { overrider : Maybe (Overrider state msg), renderer : Maybe (StateRenderer state msg) }

unions.name: LineSpec
unions.comment: Represents a single line in a chat.

unions.name: TheUpdater
unions.comment: A wrapper around Updater to prevent type recursion.

aliases.name: CustomSettings
aliases.comment:
Customizable Settings.

Make one of these with `makeSettings`, optionally customize the `customRenderers` field, and store it in your model.
aliases.type: { fontSize : Int, lines : List (LineSpec state), customRenderers : Maybe (CustomRenderers state msg), input : String, scroll : Float, attributes : ExtraAttributes msg, id : String, defaultFontSize : Int, showSizeControls : Bool, updater : TheUpdater state msg }

aliases.name: ExtraAttributes
aliases.comment:
Extra attributes for the UI components.

This is the initial value of `Settings.attributes`.

You'll usually create one by changing `defaultExtraAttributes`.
aliases.type: { chatTable : List (Attribute msg), sizeButtons : List (Attribute msg), sizeColumn : List (Attribute msg), textColumn : List (Attribute msg), textArea : List (Attribute msg) }

aliases.name: Overrider
aliases.comment: User function to override rendering of standard `LineSpec` options.
aliases.type: CustomSettings state msg -> LineSpec state -> Maybe (Html msg)

aliases.name: Sender
aliases.comment: A function to turn an input string and a `Settings` record into a `msg`.

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment:
Old, uncustomizable Settings for the chat component.

Make one of these with `makeSettings`, and store it in your model.

aliases.name: StateRenderer
aliases.type: state -> CustomSettings state msg -> Html msg

aliases.name: Updater
aliases.comment: A function to turn a `Settings` record and a `Cmd` into a `msg`.
aliases.type: CustomSettings state msg -> Cmd msg -> msg

values.name: addChat
values.comment: Add a string to the chat box.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> String -> ( CustomSettings state msg, Cmd msg )

values.name: addLineSpec
values.comment: Add a line to the chat box.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> LineSpec state -> ( CustomSettings state msg, Cmd msg )

values.name: chat
values.comment: Create a chat component.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> Html msg

values.name: customSettingsDecoder
values.comment: The JSON Decoder for `decodeCustomSettings`.
values.type: Updater state msg -> Decoder state -> Decoder (CustomSettings state msg)

values.name: customSettingsEncoder
values.comment: The JSON encoder for `encodeSettings`.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> (state -> Value) -> Value

values.name: decodeCustomSettings
values.comment:
Turn a JSON string that may contain `CustomLineSpec`s back into a `CustomSettings` record.

`Updater` is as to `makeSettings`.

The `state` decoder turns a `Value` into your state.

Restores with default `attributes`, so you'll need to change those
after decoding, if you customize them.
values.type: Updater state msg -> Decoder state -> String -> Result String (CustomSettings state msg)

values.name: decodeSettings
values.comment:
Turn a JSON string back into a `CustomSettings` record.

`Updater` is as to `makeSettings`.

Restores with default `attributes`, so you'll need to change those
after decoding, if you customize them.
values.type: Updater state msg -> String -> Result String (CustomSettings state msg)

values.name: defaultExtraAttributes
values.comment: The default value of the `Settings.attributes` property.
values.type: ExtraAttributes msg

values.name: encodeCustomSettings
values.comment:
Turn `CustomSettings` into a JSON string.

Use the `(state -> Value)` function to encode `CustomLineSpec`s.

Does not encode the `attributes` or `updater` properties.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> (state -> Value) -> String

values.name: encodeSettings
values.comment:
Turn chat `Settings` into a JSON string.

Does not encode the `attributes` or `updater` properties.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> String

values.name: inputBox
values.comment:
Create a text input control.

Args are `textSize buttonText sender settings`.

`textSize` is the width in characters of the input control.

`buttonText` is the text for the button that sends the input.

`sender` is a function to turn an input string and `settings into a`msg`.

`settings` is your `Settings` record.
values.type: Int -> String -> Sender state msg -> CustomSettings state msg -> Html msg

values.name: makeLineSpec
values.comment: Make a `LineSpec` including a message and an optional user and time.
values.type: String -> Maybe String -> Maybe Time -> LineSpec state

values.name: makeSettings
values.comment:
Make a settings record to add to your Model.

Args are `id initialFontSize showSizeControls updater`.

`id` is the Html id for the div showing the chat.

`initialFontSize` is the initial font size of the div in `pt`.

`showSizeControls` is `True` to show the font size controls to the left of the text area.

`updater` will be called to generate messages to update the settings in your Model.
values.type: String -> Int -> Bool -> Updater state msg -> CustomSettings state msg

values.name: parseOutUrl
values.comment:
Parse the first URL out of a string.

The result is `Just (prefix, url, suffix)`, if there is a URL, or `Nothing` otherwise.

"foo.com" is interpreted as a URL, but is returned as just "foo.com". You have to prepend the <http://> yourself, if that's what you need.
values.type: String -> Maybe ( String, String, String )

values.name: restoreScroll
values.comment: Restore the scroll position after restoring from a JSON string.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> Cmd msg

values.name: settingsDecoder
values.comment: The JSON Decoder for `decodeSettings`.
values.type: Updater state msg -> Decoder (CustomSettings state msg)

values.name: settingsEncoder
values.comment: The JSON encoder for `encodeSettings`.
values.type: CustomSettings state msg -> Value

values.name: styledInputBox
values.comment:
Same as `inputBox`, but takes two additional lists of attributes.

The first list is for the `input` box. The second is for the `button`.
values.type: List (Attribute msg) -> List (Attribute msg) -> Int -> String -> Sender state msg -> CustomSettings state msg -> Html msg

values.name: timeString
values.comment: Convert a `Time` to a string in the format "HH:MM"
values.type: Time -> String

values.name: timestampString
values.comment: Convert a `Time` to a string in the format "HH:MM:SS"
values.type: Time -> String
